questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: According to the CEC and Other Election Commissioners Act, 2023, which of the following is a mandatory qualification for a person to be appointed as a CEC or EC?

options.text: The person must have served as a Judge of the Supreme Court or a High Court.
The person must hold or have held a post equivalent to the Secretary to the Government of India.
The person must be a distinguished jurist in the opinion of the President.
The person must have served as the Cabinet Secretary for at least three years.

questions.explanation: The 2023 Act specifies that the person must hold or have held a post equivalent to the Secretary to the Government of India and must possess expertise in managing and conducting elections.
questions.takeaway: The eligibility criteria for CEC/EC are tied to the rank of Secretary to the Government of India.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Qualifications: For CEC and EC, the person: Must hold/have held post equivalent to Secretary to Government of India. Must possess expertise in managing and conducting elections.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: Regarding the selection process for the Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) and Election Commissioners (ECs), which of the following correctly describes the role of the Search Committee?

options.text: It is headed by the Prime Minister and recommends one final name to the President.
It is headed by the Leader of Opposition and prepares a panel of three candidates.
It is headed by the Minister of Law and Justice and prepares a panel of five candidates.
It is headed by the Cabinet Secretary and includes two members of the rank of Secretary to the Government of India.

questions.explanation: The Search Committee is headed by the Minister of Law and Justice and includes two other members (Secretary rank or higher). Its primary function is to prepare a panel of five candidates for the consideration of the Select Committee.
questions.takeaway: The Search Committee is a preliminary body led by the Law Minister to shortlist five candidates.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Search Committee Leadership: Headed by Minister of Law and Justice. Composition: Two members (Secretary rank or higher). Function: Prepares panel of 5 candidates.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: Which of the following are members of the 'Select Committee' responsible for recommending names for the appointment of the CEC and ECs to the President?

options.text: Prime Minister, Chief Justice of India, and Leader of Opposition.
Prime Minister, Leader of Opposition, and a Union Cabinet Minister nominated by the PM.
Minister of Law and Justice, Leader of Opposition, and Speaker of the Lok Sabha.
Prime Minister, Minister of Law and Justice, and two Secretaries to the Government of India.

questions.explanation: Under the 2023 Act, the Select Committee consists of the Prime Minister (Chairperson), the Leader of the Opposition in the Lok Sabha (Member), and a Union Cabinet Minister nominated by the Prime Minister (Member). The Chief Justice of India is not a member of this committee under the new Act.
questions.takeaway: The Select Committee for ECI appointments is executive-heavy, consisting of the PM, a PM-nominated Minister, and the LoP.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Select Committee Members: Prime Minister (Chairperson). Leader of Opposition (Member). Union Cabinet Minister nominated by PM (Member). Function: Recommends candidates to President.
